chapter_title.ar: بَابُ النَّهْيِ عَنِ الصَّيْدِ وَ مَا يُصْنَعُ بِهِ إِذَا أَصَابَهُ الْمُحْرِمُ وَ الْمُحِلُّ فِي الْحِلِّ وَ الْحَرَمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 106 – The Prohibition from the hunting and what to do with it when the one in Ihraam does it, and the one not in Ihraam in the non-restricted area and in the Harram

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/363)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/363)
text: لَا تَأْكُلْ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ وَ أَنْتَ حَرَامٌ وَ إِنْ كَانَ الَّذِي أَصَابَهُ مُحِلٌّ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ فِدَاءٌ مَا أَتَيْتَهُ بِجَهَالَةٍ إِلَّا الصَّيْدَ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْكَ فِيهِ الْفِدَاءَ بِجَهْلٍ كَانَ أَوْ بِعَمْدٍ.

en.hubeali: Ibn Abu Umeyr and Safwan Bin Yahya, altogether from Muawiya Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘You should not eat from the prey and you are in Ihraam, and even if the one who caught it is not in Ihraam; and there is no ransom upon you for as long you come to it out of ignorance, except for the hunting. Thus, upon you, with regards to it, is the ransom, whether it was out of ignorance or deliberate’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ibn abu ‘Umayr and from Safwan ibn Yahya all has narrated from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘You must not eat of the flesh of a prey while you are in the state of Ihram, even if the hunter is not in the state of Ihram. There is no ransom on you because of what you do out of ignorance except hunting, in which case there is ransom, regardless of being done out of ignorance or with knowledge.’”
